qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Command injection in NetworkHealthCheck via user-controlled host parameter
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns shell command construction in NetworkHealthCheck from attacker-controlled health-check host configuration, while the advisory concerns unauthenticated Core-protocol federation connection setup to an attacker-controlled broker.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Management API authorization bypass in ActiveMQServerControlImpl permits privilege escalation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets authenticated JMX/Jolokia management operations in ActiveMQServerControlImpl, not the advisory's unauthenticated Core protocol path that forces outbound Core federation to a rogue broker.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause: unauthenticated remote Core protocol use to force an outbound Core federation connection to an attacker-controlled broker, enabling possible queue message injection or exfiltration.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-fw88-pf9m-p947 describes CWE-306 in Apache ActiveMQ Artemis Core protocol federation connection handling, exploitable without authentication when Core protocol ingress and broker egress are allowed.
command_injection_network_health_check/vulnerability_report.json describes NetworkHealthCheck command construction from health-check host configuration and ProcessBuilder execution.
management_api_authorization_bypass/vulnerability_report.json describes authenticated JMX/Jolokia management MBean operations in ActiveMQServerControlImpl.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 do not apply.
